text: The surgeon opens the gastrohepatic ligament and dissects circumferentially around the hiatus. A Penrose drain is placed around the distal esophagus for retraction and identification of the GE junction. Monopolar scissors are used to dissect at the inferior border of the gastric mass, adjusting the margin to preserve normal gastric tissue.

The surgeon rotates the gastric mass laterally and continues dissection on its medial aspect. The final attachments are divided, and the mass is repositioned. The gastrotomy is evaluated to ensure it can be closed without tension and does not involve the gastroesophageal junction. The gastrotomy is closed in two layers: the first with absorbable barbed suture in a continuous fashion, and the second with braided permanent suture in an interrupted fashion.

After closing the gastrotomy, an EGD scope is inserted into the antrum and retroflexed to evaluate the closure, visible on the right side of the screen. The suture line shows no bleeding, and the GE junction is not narrowed. The stomach is desufflated, the scope is removed, and the specimen is extracted through the twelve millimeter port site, which is then closed with an ovicral suture.
